tasks.title: Persist purchase_price, engine, and mods on my_fleet
tasks.description: Extend my_fleet schema and the inline fleet entry path to store purchase_price, engine choice, and mods (fuel/co2/speed) so profitability calculations for inline-added aircraft are accurate.
tasks.details: Schema: add columns purchase_price INTEGER, engine TEXT, mod_fuel BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0, mod_co2 BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0, mod_speed BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0 to my_fleet. Migration file app/db/migrations/00X_fleet_engine_mods.sql, idempotent, update canonical schema.sql. Revise the my_fleet upsert conflict target: currently (hub, shortname) merges rows with different configs — change to (hub, shortname, engine, mod_fuel, mod_co2, mod_speed, config_hash) so distinct configs stay distinct. Inline fleet form in _fleet_entry_fields.html already collects these fields; wire them through api_routes_add to the upsert. Backfill existing rows with engine=NULL (treated as default by am4 calc). Verify route_aircraft baseline price logic still works.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: upsert with same (hub, shortname) but different engine creates two rows, not one. Integration: add route via inline path with non-default engine+mods, assert my_fleet row has correct values and profitability calc uses them (not defaults).

tasks.title: Extract compute_needs_extraction_refresh helper
tasks.description: Centralize the logic that determines whether a route is awaiting extraction (no valid route_aircraft row for the hub+dest+aircraft+config triple) into a single reusable function, used by both the add endpoint and the my_routes inventory partial.
tasks.details: Location: app/services/route_service.py, function compute_needs_extraction_refresh(hub, dest, shortname, config) -> bool. Replace inline logic in api_routes_add and in the Jinja context builder for my_routes_inventory.html. Ensures tasks 7/8 (persisting the flag to my_routes) have one place to change. Pure read query, no side effects.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: (a) valid route_aircraft row exists -> False; (b) no row for this triple -> True; (c) row exists but for different config -> True. Mock DB fixture.

tasks.title: Document merge-on-conflict decision for duplicate routes
tasks.description: Task 5's validator originally treated duplicate (hub, dest, shortname) routes as a 422 error; the implemented behavior is merge-on-conflict. Document this decision and align the validator tests.
tasks.details: Update route_validator.py docstring to explain: duplicate (hub, dest, shortname) is not an error; api_routes_add merges via ON CONFLICT on my_routes to support re-tuning an existing route's config. Update any test in tests/test_route_validator.py that asserts duplicate-is-error to instead assert duplicate-is-warning (or remove if redundant). Add a note to README or docs/decisions/ capturing the rationale.
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: validator returns a warning (not an error) for duplicate triple; api_routes_add POST with duplicate triple returns 200 and updates the existing row's config fields.
